question: US × Iran ceasefire by April 30?
narrative_context: Iran rejected US 15-point plan; FM publicly denies peace talks occurring (Al Jazeera, April 1 2026)
Active military escalation: Israel struck IRGC targets + Isfahan; Iran fired missiles at central Israel (Al Jazeera, April 1 2026)
Ceasefire resolution requires mutual public confirmation + 14 consecutive zero-action days; with 29 days remaining, timeline is impossibly tight
Pakistan backchannel exists but Iran refusing to negotiate; no serious diplomatic engagement underway (Al Jazeera, April 1 2026)
thesis: Market at ~25.5%, signal estimates ~8%. Iran has explicitly rejected negotiations. The military escalation is active (last 48 hours: major airstrikes). Even if talks restarted today, 29 days isn't enough time for serious negotiation + implementation of zero-action period. This is a near-certain NO.

Same story: Iran rejected talks, military escalation ongoing, timeline impossibly tight.
Market requires 14 zero-action days + mutual confirmation in 29 days total. With active escalation, this is fantasy.
Don't chase hope. The ground reality is clear. Bet accordingly. Track record: https://dub.sh/pb-x
